div.p: Memory Pool M3 — Compile-Time and Runtime Feature Control
• Atomic Mutex Lock • Bitmap Double-free Check • Physical Page Alignment
• All Prod Features • Callstack backtrace tracking • Leak summary on exit
• memset(0xDE) on Free • Check 0xDE on Alloc • Block scrubbing
• Header Canary (8B) • Footer Canary (8B) • Alignment Shifting
• Combined Poison + Canary • Metadata shadow-mapping • Exhaustive heap validation

Macro: POOL_DEBUG Defining POOL_DEBUG at compile-time allocates the necessary internal bitfields and enables runtime branch checks. Without this flag, all debug methods are compiled out to No-Op via preprocessor #ifdef.

ul.li: use_poison: Scrubbing frequency is determined by the poison_ratio global config.
use_canaries: Injected as 64-bit non-zero constants at the [ptr-8] and [ptr+size] relative offsets.
✓ YES : Deterministic detection on next allocator call.
✗ NO : Bug likely undetected until crash or data corruption.
Baseline : Production performance on M3 Ultra silicon.
